title: Official-source research: NYC Zoning Resolution text corpus
objective: Discover, document, and verify the official distribution channels for the complete NYC Zoning Resolution text (zoningresolution.planning.nyc.gov, any API/JSON endpoints, PDF chapter downloads, effective-date/version model, section hierarchy and cross-reference structure), following the M1-T001/M1-T003 evidence standard: live-verified channels, verbatim version/update statements, structure inventory, discrepancies, PRD 8.2 registry draft, fixture proposal, OQ ledger, no guessing
business_reason: M3 legal-corpus ingestion cannot be contracted without verified official ZR sources; feeds the rules platform (M4)
inputs: PRD.md sections 8.1 (Zoning Resolution family), 8.2 (registry fields), 7.1 (rule families the corpus must support)

docs/research/pluto-mappluto-2026-07-16.md and docs/research/zoning-features-ztldb-2026-07-16.md (accepted evidence standard: live verification, verbatim quotes, OQ ledger, NEEDS G1 RE-VERIFICATION markers)

Official starting point: https://zoningresolution.planning.nyc.gov/ (PRD 30); NYC Open Data catalog search for any Zoning Resolution datasets; s-media.nyc.gov DCP PDF hosting pattern (verified in M1-T001/T003)
outputs: docs/research/zoning-resolution-2026-07-16.md (channels, structure/hierarchy model, versioning/effective-date model, discrepancies, fixture proposal, OQ ledger, source register)

acceptance_scenarios.input: enumerate official ZR text channels (web portal, any JSON/API endpoints behind it, PDF chapter downloads, print edition statements)
acceptance_scenarios.expected: every channel evidenced by live fetch or directly read official document; any undocumented-but-official API endpoints (e.g. portal XHR JSON) recorded with exact URLs and marked for stability caveats; nothing from memory

verbatim official statements on how amendments/effective dates are published; whether historical versions are retrievable; how the portal indicates currency; CityAPA/City of Yes amendment handling noted if officially stated

acceptance_scenarios.input: structure inventory: articles/chapters/sections, definitions (12-10), tables, maps/appendices, cross-reference conventions
acceptance_scenarios.expected: hierarchy model documented from the official source; anything not directly verified goes to the OQ ledger; no guessed section semantics

acceptance_scenarios.input: cross-channel discrepancies (portal vs PDF vs any Open Data mirror)
acceptance_scenarios.expected: discrepancies surfaced with evidence; recommended PRD section-8 priority order for M3 ingestion

acceptance_scenarios.input: bot protection, dead links, JS-only content
acceptance_scenarios.expected: 403/JS-rendering limits recorded honestly as OQs with exact URLs; no content promoted to fact without direct verification

risks: The ZR portal is a JS application; underlying JSON endpoints may be technically accessible but undocumented - record them as unofficial-stability channels, never as guaranteed APIs
Legal text: no paraphrase may be presented as verbatim; quote exactly or mark as summary
KB-scale fetches only; no bulk PDF downloads (sizes recorded via HEAD where possible)
